page: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor is supported by a 480 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.

This server includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-speed local storage directly attached to the Nitro hypervisor. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 30 Gbps. The combination of DDR5 memory and local NVMe storage supports high-throughput data access, though the local storage is ephemeral, representing a standard tradeoff for instance store configurations.

In benchmark testing, the c8id.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and text processing. It achieves strong results in memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis SET operations, processing 112.86 million operations per second. The hardware is highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads, including high-performance web serving, software compilation, database hosting, and CPU-based machine learning inference, particularly for prompt processing where it registers top-tier token throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the server delivers a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in software compilation, multi-core processing, and static web serving, alongside strong database operations and Redis throughput. The instance is highly suited for compute-heavy applications, database hosting, and CPU-based machine learning inference, offering a balanced, high-density hardware profile for intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-frequency compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
3800 GB of local NVMe SSD ephemeral instance storage
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in software compilation, web serving, and multi-core processing
Optimized for high-throughput databases, Redis, and CPU-based LLM inference
